field.label: License Key
field.comment: Paste your SP-XXXX subscription key or a per-module/bundle key. Leave blank if using the Bundle key.

field.label: Issued Key (auto)
field.comment: Set automatically on first successful validation. Do not edit.

field.label: License Portal URL
field.comment: eTechFlow licensing portal endpoint. Update when your portal URL changes.

field.label: Bundle License Key
field.comment: Shared key that activates every eTechFlow module on this host.

field.label: Production Environment
field.comment: Set to No on staging/dev to bypass licensing.

group.label: Payment Settings (Stripe)
group.comment: Enter your Stripe API keys so merchants can pay for a license directly from this admin. Keys are encrypted.

field.label: Stripe Secret Key
field.comment: Your Stripe secret key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...).

field.label: Stripe Publishable Key
field.comment: Your Stripe publishable key (pk_test_... or pk_live_...).

field.label: Currency
field.comment: 3-letter ISO currency code, e.g. usd, gbp, eur. Default: usd

field.label: Enable Supplier Autoflow
field.comment: When No, all observers / cron / CLI become no-ops. Default off — you must configure slots below before turning on.

field.label: Supplier Slot Definitions
field.tooltip: One slot per line, in priority order (top = first checked). Format: label|active_attr|name_attr|cost_attr|markup_attr[|stock_source_or_qty_attr]
field.comment:
One slot per line, in priority order — top = first checked.

Format (pipe-separated):
label|active_attr|name_attr|cost_attr|markup_attr|[stock_source_or_qty_attr]

Example for a 2-supplier store:
S1|s1_active|s1|s1_cost|s1_markup
S2|s2_active|s2|s2_cost|s2_markup
The optional 6th column is used by the Stock Trigger Source below — for MSI per-slot mode put the source code (onlyda), for per-slot qty attribute mode put the attribute code (s1_qty).

Lines starting with # are treated as comments.

field.label: Stock-Dependent Supplier Names
field.tooltip: Slots whose name (case-insensitive) matches a line here get auto-toggled when stock hits 0. Other slots stay manual.
field.comment:
One supplier name per line. When a slot's name attribute resolves to one of these names AND its stock trigger source hits zero, the slot's active flag is automatically flipped to No. Other slots (drop-ship suppliers, etc.) stay manual-only.

Example:
Onlyda
OurOwnWarehouse
Match is case-insensitive (whitespace trimmed). Empty list = no slots auto-toggle.

field.label: Stock Trigger Source
field.comment:
Where the auto-toggle reads "is this slot's stock empty?":
Magento product quantity — standard StockRegistry qty. Legacy / non-MSI installs.
MSI default source — InventoryApi default source qty.
MSI per-slot source — each slot config line carries an MSI source code in column 6.
Per-slot qty attribute — each slot config line carries a product attribute code in column 6 that holds that supplier's qty.
Disabled — auto-toggle off. Repricing engine still runs when merchants manually flip slot active flags.

field.label: Price Output Target
field.comment:
Which price attribute(s) the module writes when it computes cost × (1 + markup/100):
Regular price — write to price. Straight markup, no strikethrough.
Special price only — write to special_price. Merchant manages price manually.
Special price + auto-anchored regular price — write computed value to special_price, write price = special_price × anchor_multiplier. Renders strikethrough on storefront + sale-pricing on Google Shopping (recommended for retail).

field.label: Anchor Multiplier
field.tooltip: Used when output target is special_price_with_anchor. Anchor price = special × this. 1.40 = 40% higher than special.
field.comment: Default 1.40 — regular price renders 40% higher than the auto-computed special price. Adjust per your psychological-pricing preference.

field.label: Per-Product Anchor Attribute (optional)
field.tooltip: Optional attribute code that overrides the global anchor multiplier on individual products.
field.comment: Product attribute code. When set on a product (e.g. 1.60), overrides the global anchor multiplier above for that specific product. Leave empty to always use the global value.

field.label: Price Rounding
field.comment: How prices are rounded after applying markup. 2dp matches most UK retail; 99p ending is psychological pricing (£3.27 × 1.33 → £3.99).

field.label: When No Supplier Active
field.comment: What happens to a product when every supplier slot is inactive (typically: stock has run out everywhere).

field.label: Re-toggle Active On Restock
field.comment: When Yes: if a previously-auto-toggled slot's stock comes back above 0, automatically flip it back to active and reprice from THAT slot's current cost. When No: once toggled off, the slot stays off until a merchant manually flips it back.
